meta.name: SCS-13 Self-Control Scale
meta.description: The SCS-13 is a 13-item brief version of the Self-Control Scale that measures individual differences in self-control. It assesses the ability to override or change inner responses, interrupt undesired behavioral tendencies, and refrain from acting on impulses.
meta.author: Tangney, Baumeister & Boone (2004)
meta.reference: Tangney JP, Baumeister RF, Boone AL. High self-control predicts good adjustment, less pathology, better grades, and interpersonal success. J Pers. 2004;72(2):271-324.
meta.instruction: Using the scale provided, please indicate how much each of the following statements reflects how you typically are.

questions.text: I am good at resisting temptation.
I have a hard time breaking bad habits.
I do certain things that are bad for me, if they are fun.
I refuse things that are bad for me.
I wish I had more self-discipline.
People would say that I have iron self-discipline.
Pleasure and fun sometimes keep me from getting work done.
I am able to work effectively toward long-term goals.
Sometimes I can't stop myself from doing something, even if I know it is wrong.
I often act without thinking through all the alternatives.

ranges.label: Low Self-Control
ranges.description: Your self-control score is low, suggesting significant difficulty in regulating impulses, habits, and behavior.
suggestions: Start with small, achievable goals to gradually build self-discipline
Use implementation intentions (if-then plans) to manage impulses
Remove temptations from your environment when possible
Consider working with a counselor on behavior change strategies

ranges.label: Below Average Self-Control
ranges.description: Your self-control is below average. You may sometimes struggle with impulses and maintaining discipline in certain areas.
suggestions: Identify specific triggers that undermine your self-control
Practice delayed gratification in small daily decisions
Establish consistent routines to reduce reliance on willpower
Get adequate sleep, as fatigue depletes self-control resources

ranges.label: Above Average Self-Control
ranges.description: Your self-control is above average. You generally manage impulses well and can maintain focus on your goals.
suggestions: Be mindful of situations where self-control may be depleted
Use your self-control strengths to pursue meaningful long-term goals

ranges.label: High Self-Control
ranges.description: Your self-control is high. You demonstrate strong ability to resist temptation, break bad habits, and maintain discipline.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your strong self-regulation practices
Ensure you also allow flexibility and spontaneity in your life
Use your self-control as a resource to model healthy habits for others
Remember that balance between control and relaxation is important
